subject: Review F-DRSARCH.2: unnamed
body:
## Claim Review Request: F-DRSARCH.2
**Paper:** DRS-ARCH — The Dual Reader Standard: Architecture Specification
**Claim:** unnamed
**Type:** F

### Statement
The DRS backward compatibility contract holds: any AI layer valid under schema version N remains valid under schema version N+1. New versions add fields; no version removes or renames existing required fields.

### Falsification Predicate
**FALSIFIED_IF:** EXISTS layer IN corpus_layers, EXISTS v_old IN schema_versions, EXISTS v_new IN schema_versions SUCH THAT v_new > v_old AND validates(layer, v_old) = TRUE AND validates(layer, v_new) = FALSE
**WHERE:** {'corpus_layers': 'set · dimensionless · all AI layer JSON files in the Fracttalix corpus', 'schema_versions': 'set · dimensionless · all published versions of ai-layer-schema.json (v1, v2-S48, v2-S50, v3-S49, v3-S51)', 'validates': 'function · boolean · returns TRUE iff the layer passes JSON Schema validation against the specified schema version'}
**EVALUATION:** For each AI layer in the corpus: validate against every schema version from its produced version through the latest. If any layer passes its original schema but fails a newer schema, the claim is falsified. Finite deterministic procedure bounded by layer count × schema version count.
**BOUNDARY:** Zero failures → NOT FALSIFIED (inclusive). Any single failure falsifies.
**CONTEXT:** F-DRSARCH.2 · threshold 0 · backward compatibility is the strongest contract in Section 16 because it is machine-testable, unlike lateral and forward compatibility which are governance commitments

**Derivation chain:** DRS-ARCH Section 16.2 → DRS-ARCH Section 16.6

### Requested
1. Verify the falsification predicate is logically consistent
2. Check math against known results (Strogatz, Kuznetsov, etc.)
3. Identify counterexamples or edge cases missed
4. Cross-reference against published literature
5. Provide verdict with confidence and reasoning
